channel.title: Frontion - Geopolitical News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news headlines from global and regional sources

item.title: Takaichi pushes mineral stockpiling plan at G7, seeks to bridge divides as Japan’s Iron Lady takes the stage
item.description: (Asia-Pacific) [SCMP] 🇯🇵⛏️ Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi proposed a joint critical minerals stockpiling initiative at the G7 summit to counter China’s dominance, while also welcoming the US-Iran memorandum as a step toward regional de-escalation.

item.title: Bank of Japan hikes rates to 1% — highest since 1995 — as Iran war fuels inflation risks
item.description: (Economy) [Nikkei Asia] 🇯🇵💰 The BOJ raised its benchmark rate to 1%, the highest level in 31 years, citing price pressures from the Iran war energy shock. The Nikkei topped 70,000 for the first time as markets viewed the move as confidence in economic normalization.

item.title: Trump eyes Ukraine peace deal at G7 as Zelensky joins leaders in France
item.description: (Diplomacy) [The Telegraph] 🇬🇧🇺🇦 The UK pledged enriched uranium for Ukraine’s nuclear plants and fresh sanctions on Russia as G7 leaders met Zelensky. Trump signaled optimism about a possible Ukraine breakthrough after the Iran deal momentum.

item.title: South Korea and Indonesia rein in derivatives trading to defend currencies amid energy shock
item.description: (Economy) [Nikkei Asia] 🇰🇷🇮🇩💱 Asian economies heavily reliant on imported energy are tightening oversight of speculative currency derivatives trades as the Iran war-driven oil disruption rattles forex markets.

item.title: Ukrainian drone attack sets oil depot ablaze in Russia’s Krasnodar region near Crimea
item.description: (Ukraine-Russia War) [Kyiv Independent] 🇺🇦🔥 A drone attack sparked a major fire at an oil depot in the village of Poltavskaya in Russia’s southern Krasnodar region, forcing the closure of a road connecting to annexed Crimea. The main fire was later extinguished.

item.title: European leaders warn Trump on Iran deal risks, push Ukraine rethink at G7 summit
item.description: (Diplomacy) [Reuters] 🇪🇺 European leaders told Trump at the G7 summit in Evian that a superficial interim Iran deal risks entrenching Tehran's nuclear and missile programs, while pressing him to rethink his Ukraine strategy and stand behind Kyiv.

item.title: G7 leaders convene in France awaiting clarity on US-Iran agreement terms
item.description: (Diplomacy) [CNN] 🇫🇷 World leaders gathered at the G7 summit in Evian-les-Bains are seeking details on the US-Iran deal, with the Iran war and its implications dominating the agenda alongside trade disputes and the Ukraine conflict.

item.title: Bank of Japan raises key interest rate to 1%, highest in three decades
item.description: (Economy) [AP News] 🇯🇵 The Bank of Japan raised its benchmark rate to 1%, a 31-year high, citing inflation pressures from the Middle East conflict and a weak yen. The Nikkei 225 briefly topped 70,000 for the first time before retreating.

item.title: Ebola risk for World Cup is extremely low, but US hospitals are ready, experts say
item.description: (Health) [Reuters] ⚽ US infectious disease experts say the risk of an Ebola case arriving during the 2026 World Cup is low but not zero, and American hospitals are prepared to respond if needed amid Congo's ongoing outbreak.

item.title: Oil rebounds on concerns about Iran deal details and Hormuz reopening delay
item.description: (Energy) [Reuters] 🛢️ Oil prices rebounded on concerns that the preliminary US-Iran peace agreement lacks detail and that resuming supply through the Strait of Hormuz could take longer than expected due to mine-clearance operations.

item.title: China embraces Myanmar president as former junta chief seeks legitimacy
item.description: (Asia-Pacific) [Reuters] 🇲🇲🇨🇳 Myanmar President Min Aung Hlaing's state visit to China gives the former junta chief a significant diplomatic boost as he seeks to consolidate power following a widely condemned election. Xi Jinping hosted the five-day visit in Beijing.

item.title: Iran's US diaspora divided between protest and cheering at World Cup
item.description: (Middle East) [AP News] ⚽🇮🇷 Iranian Americans are torn over Iran's participation in the 2026 World Cup, with some planning to watch the game and others protesting outside the LA stadium where the team played its opening match amid the backdrop of the US-Iran war.

item.title: How the US-Israel war with Iran unfolded over 108 turbulent days
item.description: (Geopolitics) [TRT World] 🇮🇷⚔️ A comprehensive timeline of the key moments in the 108-day US-Israel war with Iran that claimed thousands of lives and fundamentally reshaped the Middle East security landscape, from the first strikes to the tentative peace deal.

item.title: G7 leaders to meet Zelensky as Trump signals possible Ukraine breakthrough
item.description: (Europe) [France 24] 🇺🇦🇪🇺 G7 leaders will hold talks with President Zelensky on Tuesday as Trump suggested a possible breakthrough on Ukraine peace, shifting focus from the Iran deal to the ongoing Russia-Ukraine conflict at the summit in France.

item.title: Social media overtakes traditional outlets as worlds leading news source for first time
item.description: (Technology) [France 24] 📱🌍 News consumers worldwide are increasingly turning to social media and video platforms for information, overtaking traditional outlets for the first time according to a Reuters Institute report, raising concerns about misinformation and media sustainability.

item.title: Japan PM Takaichi proposes G7 framework for stockpiling critical minerals
item.description: (Asia-Pacific) [Japan Times] 🇯🇵⛏️ Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi pitched a plan at the G7 summit for coordinating critical mineral stockpiles among member nations and like-minded countries, aiming to reduce dependency on China for materials vital to defense and green energy industries.

item.title: Netanyahu and Trump on collision course as US-Iran deal leaves Israel sidelined
item.description: (Middle East) [Reuters] 🇮🇱 Israeli PM Netanyahu has repeatedly clashed with Trump over Israel’s refusal to constrain its pursuit of Hezbollah in Lebanon, where a cessation of hostilities is a key Iranian demand in the new US-Iran peace deal.

item.title: Russia slashes fuel quality rules as Ukrainian attacks squeeze domestic supplies
item.description: (Ukraine-Russia War) [Reuters] 🇷🇺 Russia is allowing refineries to produce lower-specification fuel for the domestic market as Ukrainian drone strikes on energy infrastructure create growing shortages, Kommersant reports.

item.title: Ebola cases top 800 in Congo as aid groups warn outbreak may be larger than reported
item.description: (Africa) [Japan Times] 🇨🇩 The outbreak is already the third largest Ebola epidemic on record and the biggest caused by the Bundibugyo strain, with aid organizations warning the true scale may exceed official figures.

item.title: Drone debris causes fire at Russian oil depot near Crimea
item.description: (Ukraine-Russia War) [U.S. News] 💥 Drone debris sparked a fire at an oil depot in Russia’s southern Krasnodar region, authorities said, closing a local road connecting the damaged area to the Crimean peninsula.

item.title: IMF Chief Says No Global Slowdown Yet but Risks Remain High
item.description: (Economy) [Reuters] 💰🌍 IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva said the world economy is weathering the shock of the Middle East war with no signs yet of a global slowdown, but warned that risks remain elevated amid ongoing geopolitical uncertainties.

item.title: Lukashenko urges both sides to compromise to end Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: (Ukraine-Russia War) [Straits Times] 🇧🇾🇺🇦 Belarusian President Lukashenko, one of Putin's closest allies, publicly called for compromise from both Russia and Ukraine to end the war, a notable shift in rhetoric from a key Kremlin ally.

item.title: Oil companies give Venezuela a second look amid Middle East chaos
item.description: (Energy) [Politico] 🇻🇪🛢️ Trump's Middle East policy disruptions are creating an unexpected beneficiary: Venezuela. Oil companies experiencing FOMO are reconsidering Venezuelan opportunities as Mideast supply uncertainties persist.

item.title: Senate Republicans demand a say on Trumps Iran deal
item.description: (US Politics) [Politico] 🏛️🇺🇸 Republican lawmakers said they are eager to examine the fine print of the US-Iran MOU, though there is a high bar to overturning any final agreement. The move signals congressional assertiveness on war powers.

item.title: Trump dismisses reports of $300B Iran reconstruction fund as fake news
item.description: (US-Iran War) [The Hill] 🇺🇸🇮🇷 President Trump pushed back on reports that a $300 billion reconstruction fund for Iran would be part of the peace agreement, casting doubt on one of the most controversial rumored elements of the deal.
